task: I need to send a brief reply to a company about a notice I received, and I want to make sure I don’t include anything unnecessary. Can you help me shape it so it covers the point without opening the door to extra back-and-forth?

prefs.explanation: She is unlikely to appreciate hedging or soft-pedaling when she asks for help. Her life pattern is to cut through confusion quickly and verify what matters, so direct answers fit her style. Overly delicate wording would probably feel evasive or wasteful to her.

prefs.short: Prefers concise answers over long explanations
prefs.explanation: She tends to act on practical information and would likely prefer the core point first. Long-winded responses would feel like clutter unless she specifically asks for detail. She seems to value efficiency in communication the same way she values efficiency in paperwork and routines.

prefs.short: Likes organized checklists and clear steps
prefs.explanation: She responds best to information that is easy to verify and follow in order. A structured format would match her record-keeping habits and reduce the chance of missing something important. Loose, rambling prose would be less useful to her than a clean list or step-by-step outline.

She is not looking for warmth that feels performative or sentimental. A steady, neutral tone would likely feel more trustworthy because it avoids trying to manipulate her mood. She probably dislikes being treated as fragile or emotionally steered.

She is very protective of her personal space and information, so she would likely want interactions that do not pry. She would probably be uncomfortable with unnecessary requests for private details or casual data-sharing. A restrained approach that asks only for what is needed would fit her well.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, plain version you can use:

> I received your notice. Please send the missing details and any supporting documents, including the dates, amounts, and basis for the notice. I’m not able to respond further until I have that in writing.

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little firmer,
- more neutral/polite, or
- tailored to the specific notice you got.

per_pref.reason: Very direct and no-nonsense: "here’s a short, plain version you can use" and the draft gets straight to the point.
Concise and efficient; it gives a brief template without extra explanation.
Well organized with a ready-to-use quote and a short bullet list of optional tweaks.
Mostly matter-of-fact and neutral, e.g. "short, plain version" and "more neutral/polite."
Strongly minimizes disclosure and back-and-forth: "Please send the missing details..." and "I’m not able to respond further until I have that in writing."

turns.agent_response:
That works. Here’s the tightened version:

> I received your notice. Please send the specific basis for it, including the dates, amounts, and any supporting documents. I will review it once I have that in writing.

If you want, I can make it even more minimal or firmer.

per_pref.reason: Very direct and no-nonsense: "Here’s the tightened version" and the reply gets straight to the request without hedging.
Concise and efficient; it gives a brief draft and only adds one short offer: "If you want, I can make it even more minimal or firmer."
Uses a clean, easy-to-follow structure with a quoted draft. Not a checklist, but it is organized and step-like.
Matter-of-fact and neutral throughout; no warmth or emotional framing, just practical wording.
Strongly minimizes disclosure and avoids unnecessary personal details; it explicitly asks only for "the specific basis," "dates, amounts, and any supporting documents."
